commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1a5dca094de9a6ce8be0ab4b687acecd08a298ff)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds memory-lancedb filtering and sanitization for inbound envelope metadata, adds a public `plugin-sdk/chat-channel-ids` subpath, updates SDK export metadata, and expands memory-lancedb tests.

PR surface: Source +342, Tests +486, Config +4, Generated 0, Other +1. Total +833 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current `formatInboundEnvelope` leaves direct-message bodies unprefixed, while the proposed sanitizer strips the first colon-prefixed token after any envelope bracket. A direct envelope like `[telegram alice] TODO: remember this` would store `remember this`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human PR review because the contributor proof gate and public SDK API decision are not safe for an automated repair lane, even though the sanitizer defect itself is concrete.

target.title: fix(memory-lancedb): reject envelope metadata sludge (incl. marker-free shapes)
target.body:
Supersedes #73787, which was auto-closed by clawsweeper for the timestamp-free envelope coverage gap and could not be reopened after the fix was force-pushed (GitHub's `reopenPullRequest` returns `UNPROCESSABLE` once the head ref is rewritten).

## Summary

Closes the marker-free envelope gap. `formatInboundEnvelope` (`src/auto-reply/envelope.ts`) drops `+<elapsed>`, host, ip, and the absolute timestamp when their inputs are absent, leaving the minimum real shape `[<channel> <from>] <body>`. The previous `looksLikeEnvelopeSludge` keyed only on elapsed/date markers, so a contaminated message like `[telegram alice] hello` slipped past the auto-capture filter.

## Changes

- New SDK subpath `openclaw/plugin-sdk/chat-channel-ids` re-exports `CHAT_CHANNEL_ORDER` from `src/channels/ids.ts` as `BUNDLED_CHAT_CHANNEL_IDS`. The list is the same one the bundled channel config metadata is derived from, so the detector and the inbound-envelope formatter cannot drift.
- `looksLikeEnvelopeSludge` adds a second regex anchored on `BUNDLED_CHAT_CHANNEL_IDS` to recognize `[<channel-id> <from>(<more parts>)?]` shapes regardless of whether elapsed/date markers are present. The existing marker-aware regex still covers third-party channels not in the bundled list.
- `sanitizeForMemoryCapture` falls back to the new regex when the marker-aware regex misses, so the auto-capture path surfaces a clean body for marker-free envelopes too.
- Tests cover: marker-free envelopes (`[telegram alice] hello world`), case-insensitive channel match, markdown link false-positive guard `[text](url)`, unknown bracketed label false-positive guard `[note] ...`, bare `[channel]` (no from label) false-positive guard, and multi-line envelope-prefixed messages.

## Verification

- `pnpm build` -- clean.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-lancedb` -- `Test Files  2 passed (2)`, `Tests  108 passed (108)`.
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen` -- regenerated SHA committed at `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`.
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs` -- OK.
- Plugin SDK contract tests (`src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts`, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`) -- green.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: timestamp-free `formatInboundEnvelope` outputs were not being stripped before auto-capture; a marker-free envelope like `[telegram alice] hello` would pass the gate and persist envelope bytes into the LanceDB store.
- **Real environment tested**: vitest against `extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts` in this branch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-lancedb`.
- **Evidence after fix**: `Test Files  2 passed (2)` / `Tests  108 passed (108)` (was 101 before the new cases). Marker-free, marker-present, markdown-link, unknown-label, and multi-line envelope tests all green.
- **Observed result after fix**: filtered envelopes match the formatter contract for all supported envelope shapes, including marker-free `[<channel-id> <from>] body`. Channel-id list is sourced from the canonical bundled registry, so any newly bundled channel becomes a recognized envelope prefix automatically.
- **What was not tested**: live Telegram capture round-trip on the mac-mini gateway.
target.author: amittell

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
